title: If I Were You – Hard VSAQs

questions.question: Why does the intruder want to assume Gerrard’s identity?
questions.answer: To escape the police and live freely.

questions.question: How does Gerrard psychologically control the intruder?
questions.answer: By pretending to be a criminal himself.

questions.question: Why does Gerrard say the intruder cannot kill him?
questions.answer: Because it would not help him escape.

questions.question: What literary device is used when Gerrard pretends to welcome the intruder?
questions.answer: Irony.

questions.question: What does Gerrard’s calm behaviour reveal?
questions.answer: His intelligence and confidence.

questions.question: Why does Gerrard invent a story about being a criminal?
questions.answer: To gain time and confuse the intruder.

questions.question: How does dialogue help build suspense in the play?
questions.answer: It reveals the intruder’s dangerous plan gradually.

questions.question: Why does the intruder hesitate after hearing Gerrard’s story?
questions.answer: He becomes unsure about killing him.

questions.question: What weakness of the intruder helps Gerrard succeed?
questions.answer: His overconfidence.

questions.question: What role does intelligence play in the story?
questions.answer: It helps Gerrard defeat the intruder.

questions.question: Why does Gerrard suggest leaving the cottage together?
questions.answer: To trick the intruder into moving closer to the cupboard.

questions.question: What does the intruder represent in the play?
questions.answer: Crime and violence.

questions.question: What does Gerrard represent in the play?
questions.answer: Intelligence and courage.

questions.question: Why does the play rely heavily on dialogue?
questions.answer: Because it is written as a drama.

questions.question: What lesson about danger does the play teach?
questions.answer: Calm thinking can help overcome danger.

questions.question: How does Gerrard turn the intruder’s plan against him?
questions.answer: By trapping him in the cupboard.

questions.question: Why is the intruder’s plan flawed?
questions.answer: Because he underestimates Gerrard.

questions.question: What dramatic effect does irony create in the play?
questions.answer: It increases suspense and humour.

questions.question: What theme is highlighted through Gerrard’s victory?
questions.answer: Intelligence is stronger than violence.

questions.question: Why is Gerrard considered clever?
questions.answer: Because he defeats the intruder without violence.

questions.question: How does the play maintain tension?
questions.answer: Through threatening dialogue.

questions.question: What message does the title indirectly suggest?
questions.answer: One should think wisely before acting.

questions.question: What does the ending emphasise about justice?
questions.answer: Crime ultimately fails.

questions.question: Why is Gerrard’s strategy effective?
questions.answer: Because it confuses the intruder.

questions.question: What final action ensures safety in the play?
questions.answer: Calling the police.
